PG Programme in
 Human Resources & Organisation Strategy

A 16-month full-time PGP that blends core business foundations with advanced learning in human
resources, culture, and organisational strategy.

Apply Now

PG Programme in
Human Resources & Organisation Strategy (HR & OS)

Format

Full Time

On Campus
(Opt-in residential)

Commencement Date

June 2026

Round 4 Deadline: 11th April '26

Eligibility

Bachelor’s Degree (Any)

Fresh Graduates (0–11 Months)
Early Professionals (1-5 Years)

Duration

16 Months

Optional International &
Domestic Immersions, LIVE
Projects & International Internships

Apply Now
  • Highlights
  • Curriculum
  • Immersions
  • Admissions & Fees
  • Career Prospects
  • Faculty & Student Stories

Learn by Doing
Not Just by Sitting in Lectures

At Masters’ Union, we believe that HR today is no longer about hiring, policies, or employee engagement. It’s about designing organisations, scaling cultures, and building teams that drive business growth.

Enabler To Builder

Move Beyond Traditional HR-
Learn to Build Organisations

A programme for future HR leaders who want to shape culture,
design org. structures, and become strategic partners in driving
business performance.

Industry-Led Curriculum

An Entire Term Taught by
Leaders from Aon

Learn from senior leaders who design High-impact
organisations and graduate with industry and coaching
certifications.

Immersive Exposure

Business-First, Taught by
Real-World Leaders

Learn from CHROs, founders, operators, and MU
faculty on how to connect HR strategy with business goals
through hands-on projects, sprints, and live company
work.

Culture Diagnostics

The Culture Audit Lab-
Diagnose a Broken Culture

Use interviews, analytics, and employee sentiment dashboards to identify culture gaps, then present a transformation roadmap to early and late stage founders.

Hiring Sprints

Lead a Talent
Acquisition Sprint

Source candidates, design assessments, conduct
interviews, coordinate hiring panels, and drive final
selections as part of live projects with companies.

Employer Branding Lab

Create an Employer
Branding Campaign

Study and craft how organisations attract the best candidates through career experiences, social content, video assets, and conversion pipelines with real capital at stake.

LIVE Industry Projects

Work Directly with HR Team of
Start-ups and Large Companies

Design org. structures, hiring charters, compensation &
ESOP models, and culture playbooks – present directly
to founders & CHROs.

Dropshipping Challenge

Generate 1Cr+ Revenue - Learn
the Fundamentals of Business

From running marketing campaigns to managing supply
chains, build your own e-commerce store and compete for
revenue.

Domestic & International Immersions

Learning Beyond
Classrooms

Build a global perspective, learn from renowned faculty and industry leaders, and gain first-hand exposure to international business through a two-week immersive experience at a leading global institute.

Developed and Taught in
Partnership with Aon

7 co-designed industry-first courses taught by consultants and professionals from Aon and a Capstone Project. Domestic and international internships + graduate with a globally-recognised HR certificate from Aon.

100+ Empaneled
Industry Experts

Our course is taught by CXOs and MDs of topmost companies such
as Amazon, Airtel, PayPal, Mastercard, Swiggy, Samsung etc.

Designed for Next Generation
of HR Professionals

Designed for
Next Generation of HR Professionals

Learn how businesses grow and how people, tech, data, and culture drive
performance shaping future HR leaders as strategic partners.

Hands-on Real-World Learnings from
Top Faculties, CHROs & HR Heads

Learn from full-time faculty, CHROs and HR leaders who build
strong cultures by aligning business, tech, and people strategy.

1

Live business projects with partner firms

2

Direct mentorship from industry leaders

3

Industry-led workshops and immersive projects

HR Leaders of Tomorrow:
Business Strategists, Not Process Managers

The curriculum spans 8 terms.

1

Terms 1-2: Focusing on Core Business Foundations

2

Terms 3-5: Specialisations in Human Resource & Organisation Strategy

3

Terms 6-8: Dedicated to live projects & consulting practice

Intern With Top Companies
for 3 Months

Work with top startups, tech firms, and business leaders to gain industry experience.

1

Design modern organisations & talent strategies

2

Learn to use people analytics to drive decisions

3

Build future-ready leadership practice

Hands-on Real-World Learnings from
Top Faculties, CHROs & HR Heads

strong cultures by aligning business, tech, and people strategy.

1

HR Leaders of Tomorrow:
Business Strategists, Not Process Managers

The curriculum spans 8 terms.

1

Terms 1-2: Focusing on Core Business Foundations

2

Terms 3-5: Specialisations in Human Resource & Organisation Strategy

3

Terms 6-8: Dedicated to live projects & consulting practice

Intern With Top
Companies for 3 Months

Work with top startups, tech firms, and business leaders to gain industry experience.

1

Design modern organisations & talent strategies

2

Learn to use people analytics to drive decisions

3

Build future-ready leadership practice

Why HR & OS, Why Now?

The world of work is transforming faster than ever. With AI, hybrid teams, and global
mobility reshaping organisations, HR has become a core driver of business strategy.
India produces ~5 lakh MBAs, yet under 5% specialise in HR, despite growing
demand for leaders skilled in analytics, culture, and people strategy. Globally, the
CHRO is now the #2 most in-demand C-suite role after CTO, reflecting HR’s rising
importance.

The Masters’ Union PGP in Human Resources & Organisation Strategy (HR&OS)
develops future-ready HR leaders trained in business fundamentals, people
analytics, and real-world organisational design to shape the future of work.

16 Student-Led Clubs:
Connecting & Building Beyond Class

Masters’ Union hosts 16 student-led clubs that go beyond classroom
learning, helping students connect, unwind, and grow through shared
interests and hands-on experiences.

  • AI and Analytics Wing
  • MU Consulting Club
  • CommerceX Club
  • Sports Club
  • Creator Club
  • Socio Cultural Club
  • Fintech Club
  • FOCOS Club
  • B2B Club
  • Third Block Club
  • Prodman Club
  • The Marketing Society
  • EdTech Club
  • SEWA Club
  • Belong Club
  • Shastra Club

AI and Analytics Wing

AI & Analytics Wing empowers students to apply data-driven thinking via hands-on projects, simulations, and case challenges.

Mu Consulting Club

MUCC equips students with real-world consulting skills through case prep, live projects, and collaborative learning.

CommerceX Club

CommerceX explores operations, supply chains, and efficiency through workshops, speaker sessions, and industry visits.

Sports Club

Sports Club fosters fitness, unity, and resilience through campus sports and represents MU with pride at intercollege tournaments.

Creator Club

The Creators Club unites students passionate about content and storytelling to collaborate, experiment, and create impactful ideas.

Socio Cultural Club

The Socio-Cultural Club celebrates art, culture, and community through vibrant events and performances that go beyond the classroom.

Fintech Club

The FinTech Club explores innovations shaping modern finance through industry talks and projects that build practical understanding.

FOCOS Club

The club supports students aiming for Founder’s Office and Chief of Staff roles through engaging, gamified learning and guided practice.

B2B Club

B2B Club empowers students to explore MSMEs and family businesses, highlighting sectors beyond startups and placements.

Third Block Club

TBC Club builds a hands-on community exploring blockchain, digital assets, and Web3 through interactive workshops and discussions.

Prodman Club

The PM Club builds future product leaders who bridge business, design, and tech through strategy sessions and hands-on projects.

The Marketing Society

Marketing Society helps students learn and experiment with brand strategy, consumer insights, and storytelling in action.

EdTech Club

EdTech Club drives innovation in learning by exploring how technology transforms education and the startup ecosystem.

SEWA Club

SEWA Club drives student-led social impact and community work, inspiring members to give back beyond academics and careers.

Belong Club

Belong builds a culture of empathy and inclusion at MU, creating a safe space where every student feels seen, supported, and connected.

Shastra Club

Shastra Club brings ancient Indian knowledge to life, exploring how timeless ideas can shape modern leadership and management.

Shastra Club

Shastra Club brings ancient Indian knowledge to life, exploring how timeless ideas can shape modern leadership and management.

Shastra Club

Shastra Club brings ancient Indian knowledge to life, exploring how timeless ideas can shape modern leadership and management.

Glimpse of Student Life

Enabling You to Excel
Inside and Outside the Classroom

Business Foundations

Build core business skills in economics, finance, marketing, and communication to lead people strategy with a strong business perspective.

  • In Class: Core Courses
  • Out Class
  • Communication and Self-Development: How to Give an Inspiring Speech
  • Finance & Fintech: How to Read and Analyse Financial Statements
  • Sales & Marketing: How to Advertise Without Spending Money
  • Management & Strategy: How to Work Effectively in Teams
  • Product & Tech: How to Set Up an E-commerce Website
  • AI & ML: How to Master Prompt Engineering to Leverage Generative AI
  • Liberal Arts: Shifting World Order & World Politics After 9/11 and Covid
  • Dropshipping Challenge: From running marketing campaigns to managing supply chains, students build their own websites and compete for revenue

Strategy and Growth

Learn strategy, analytics, and operations through hands-on problem solving and live simulations of workforce and organisational decisions.

  • In Class: Core Courses
  • Out Class
  • Communication & Self Development: How to Manage Personal Finances?
  • Finance & Fintech: How to Create an MIS for a Business
  • Sales & Marketing: How to Execute CRO and Increase AOV
  • Management & Strategy: How a Country's Economy Works
  • Product & Tech: How to Develop a Product Mindset
  • AI & ML: How LLMs & AI Actually Work
  • Liberal Arts: How Countries Grow & Make Money
  • Pre-Seed Cheque Challenge: Validate and present your startup idea to fight for 15+ Lakhs in pre-seed funding and build your Minimum Viable Product (MVP).
  • LIVE Industry Immersion: Visit HQs of companies & startups to understand their HR processes.

The Business of People

Develop expertise across people analytics, employment law, HR tech, and organisational design to influence enterprise-level decisions.

Developed and taught by Aon

  • In Class: Core Courses
  • Out Class
  • Strategic Talent Acquisition & Employer Branding: How to build winning hiring strategies and employer brands that attract top talent.
  • Learning & Capability Development: How to design learning systems that build future-ready skills and capabilities.
  • Total Rewards 101: How to structure compensation and benefits to drive performance and retention.
  • People Analytics & Data-Driven Decision Making: How to use people data and analytics to make informed workforce decisions.
  • Leveraging Technology & Generative AI in HR: How to leverage HR technology and Generative AI to improve efficiency and outcomes.
  • Talent Management & Retention Strategies: How to identify, develop, and retain high-impact talent across the organisation.
  • VIP - MVP: Transform classroom frameworks into execution-ready talent and culture strategies.
  • Lead a Talent Acquisition Sprint: Source candidates, design assessments, conduct interviews, coordinate hiring panels, and drive final selections as part of live projects with companies.
  • HR Capstone Project: Solve a real-world HR challenge by applying end-to-end people strategy in a live business context.

People Strategy and Change Leadership

Develop strong capabilities in people strategy and change leadership to build agile, high-performing organisations that drive measurable business results.

  • In Class: Core Courses
  • Out Class
  • Long-Term & Short-Term Incentives Design: How to design incentive plans that align rewards with business performance.
  • HR Business Partnering & Change Management:: How to partner with business leaders to drive change and transformation.
  • Organisational Psychology & Leadership Behaviour: How to apply behavioural science to build effective leaders and teams.
  • HR in Startups vs Large Enterprises: How to adapt HR practices across startups and large enterprises at different growth stages.
  • Organisation design and scaling culture: How to design organisational structures and cultures that scale with growth.
  • VIP - GTM: Craft a powerful GTM strategy that showcases your edge, ensures smooth market entry, and delivers real value to users.
  • Design an Employer Branding Strategy: Build career pages, social content, recruitment videos, and talent funnels. Use real funds to execute your plan.

Strategic HR Leadership and Future of Work

Develop strategic HR leadership capabilities to design future-ready organisations powered by data, technology, and agile workforce models.

  • In Class: Core Courses
  • Out Class
  • Future of Work: AI, Automation & Workforce 2040: How to prepare organisations for tech-led shifts.
  • HR Leadership in the Boardroom: How to influence strategic decisions with people's insights.
  • Legal, Ethics & DEI in Modern Workplaces: How to build compliant, fair, inclusive, and aligned work places with global standards.
  • HR Consulting & Transformation: How to diagnose people's challenges, redesign processes, and deliver organisation-wide change.
  • VIP- PMF: Sharpen your product, reduce CAC with smarter strategy, and demonstrate genuine user stickiness.
  • Culture Audit Lab: Use interviews, analytics, and employee sentiment dashboards to identify culture gaps. Then present a transformation roadmap to early and late stage founders.
  • Optional Domestic or International Immersion: Experience hands-on learning through national and international immersions that offer real-world industry exposure and global business insights.

Live Industry Project/Internship/ICF Coaching Certification

Work directly with industry leaders to design and deliver impactful people and change initiatives across organisations.

  • In Class: Core Courses
  • Out Class
  • Sports Finance & Revenue Models: How to understand valuations, P&Ls, salaries, transfer fees, and revenue levers.
  • Data-Driven Decision-Making in Sport: How to use dashboards, metrics, and models for high-stakes decisions.
  • Sports Law, Ethics & Governance: How to navigate contracts, regulations, athlete rights, and league governance.
  • Future of Sports & Gaming: How AI, AR/VR, wearables, and new tech will reshape the next decade.
  • Leadership in High-Performance Teams: How to lead athletes, creators, and sports organisations under pressure.
  • People Strategy Capstone (LIVE company project) .
  • HR Consulting Lab (Advisory sprint with CHROs) .
  • Culture Audit Lab & Reflection.
  • International Coaching & Mentorship Certification (ICF Aligned).

Experience Learning Via National and
International Immersions

Our Immersion Programs offer students hands-on exposure to leading business practices in India and around the world. Structured around thematic tracks, students choose their focus area and gain industry experience, real-world insights, and cultural fluency.

OFF CAMPUS

Step Into the World’s Most Dynamic
Markets and Classrooms

Business management meets hands-on experience, giving you real-world
skills that truly stand out.

INSEAD (Paris)

Learn how strategy meets innovation through 4 sessions on Blue Ocean Strategy, 3 on sustainable innovation, & 3 on stakeholder engagement along with company visits to Schneider Electric, Rungis Market, and leading Paris-based startups.

NUS (Singapore)

Dive into Singapore’s startup ecosystem through 10 sessions on entrepreneurship & venture capital, 5 on blockchain & digital finance, & 5 on AI & innovation, complemented by company visits & simulation exercises.

Babson College (USA)

Discover how ventures grow from idea to impact through sessions on entrepreneurship, innovation, & leadership, with company visits to Boston’s leading startups & accelerators.

SDA Bocconi (Italy)

Gain a global perspective through 6 sessions on strategy and decision-making, 4 on innovation & tech, & 4 on sustainability. Company visits to Dallara Automobili & a balsamic vinegar producer in Modena.

NBS (Singapore)

Master the dynamics of venture capital & AI-driven business through 2 sessions on growth strategies & 3 on AI & business, complemented by company visits to leading Singapore-based enterprises.

WBS (United Kingdom)

Discover the future of sustainable business through 3 sessions on sustainability & ESG, 2 on innovation & design thinking, & 1 on creative leadership, plus company visits to Morgan Motors & leading UK enterprises.

ESCP (Germany)

Experience Berlin’s vibrant innovation landscape through 3 sessions on entrepreneurship, 2 on digital marketing, & 2 on mobility & smart industries, with exclusive company visits to Sony, BMW Motorradwerke, & MotionLab Berlin.

Foster School of Business (USA)

Learn the art of AI-led transformation through 2 sessions on AI and technology, 2 on automation & markets, & 1 on leadership, plus company visits to Boeing & Starbucks & a case challenge.

With the Bharat Immersion, Spend a Full Term Traveling Across India’s Business Hubs

We believe that the best classrooms have no walls and with our
Bharat Immersion, you’ll spend Term 3 traveling across India, meeting
entrepreneurs, factories, and markets that move the nation. You earn
credits as you learn how business is done on the ground.

7,000 KM, 20+ Cities.
One Mission- to Learn How India
Does Business

From Chandni Chowk in Delhi to Adani Enterprises in Ahmedabad. From
Dharma Productions in Mumbai to the distilleries in Goa– this is
business education, reimagined.

Hub 1

Delhi NCR

Visit Chandni Chowk to trace retail heritage, explore D2C brands, experience Parliament, ECI, NDMA and attend 9 CXO sessions.

Hub 2

Jalandhar

Uncover how ITC manufactures, how NIVIA went global, explore agriculture manufacturing at Sonalika, attend 5 CXO sessions and witness large-scale education at LPU.

Hub 3

Mundra & Ahmedabad

Explore 3 Adani enterprises covering Ports, Wilmar, and Solar, tour Amul's dairy factory, engage with IIM Ahmedabad Ventures, and attend 4 CXO sessions.

Hub 4

Mumbai

Unravel what goes behind iconic names like Dharma, the Dabbawalas and Godrej, gain financial insights from RBI, BSE/NSE, and HDFC and attend CXO sessions.

Hub 5

Goa

Cruise to Goa on Cordelia, explore iron mines and a Feni distillery, and immerse in Fado music, Portuguese culture, and Goa's nightlife.

Hub 6

Bengaluru

Learn to build winning e-commerce strategies, explore aerospace innovation and understand how IT services became India's largest export success.

Hub 7

Darjeeling

Experience a rural immersion in Indo-Nepal villages and visit Darjeeling tea estates to learn brand building, culture, and sustainability.

Hub 8

Lucknow

Explore Uttar Pradesh's defense manufacturing, global luxury embroidery, and meet government leaders shaping a trillion-dollar economy.

The immersions listed are indicative and may change based on partner availability and unforeseen circumstances.

OFF CAMPUS

1 Day Immersions

Business management meets hands-on experience, giving you real-world
skills that truly stand out.

CARS24

Experience how Cars24 is transforming India’s used-car market through data, technology, & customer trust.

ShipRocket

Step inside India’s leading logistics tech startup powering the D2C revolution & learn how innovation fuels e-commerce growth at scale.

Nivia

Witness how Nivia built an Indian sports brand competing globally through innovation in manufacturing, branding, & grassroots engagement.

Visit International Trade Fairs to Learn the Business of
Manufacturing, Supply-Chains, and Global Commerce

Learn by building AI applications to solve real world problems in
e-commerce, healthcare, education and fintech. Each year includes an AI tool development in sem 1 followed by real-world industry application in sem 2

China’s Canton Fair
Network in the GCC Region

Exhibit at the World's Largest Trade
Event - China's Canton Fair

Visit the epicentre of global trade, meet 10 lakh+ customers and suppliers from more than 150 countries, and strike deals to increase your profitability.

Explore Global Giants in the
GCC Region

Discover how Fortune 500 companies and start-ups are reshaping the GCC region. Visit giants like Emirates and Aramco, and engage with executives driving innovation.

Enrollment Checklist
Admissions Criteria

01.
Academics

01.
Academics

Bachelor’s degree (any), Fresh Graduates (0–11 months) & Early Professionals (1-5+ years)

02.
Aptitude Score

02.
Aptitude Score

Scores in Masters’ Union MU-BAAT test, or submit scores in CAT/GMAT.

03.
Extra Curricular Activities

03.
Extra Curricular Activities

Applications would be evaluated for extra curricular achievements and projects.

04.
Communication & Creativity

04.
Communication & Creativity

We will assess these qualities through application details, in-person or online interviews.

Who is an Ideal Candidate?

Aspiring HR leaders who want to go beyond traditional roles- passionate about building culture, shaping organisations, and driving business outcomes.

Early-career professionals seeking to strengthen their strategic, analytical, and people leadership skills.

Candidates who view HR as a business function interested in people analytics, organisation design, and leveraging data and technology to solve workforce challenges.

Application
Timeline & Process

Rounds Deadline MU-BAAT Test Personal Interview Application Fees Commencement Action

Round 2 16th Feb 2026 Upon submission of your application Within one week of your MU BAAT submission INR 500/- June 2026 closed
Round 3 16th March 2026 Upon submission of your application Within one week of your MU BAAT submission INR 500/- June 2026 closed
Round 4 11th April 2026 Upon submission of your application Within one week of your MU BAAT submission INR 500/- June 2026 Apply Now

Blueprint to a
Standout Application

Your application is more than just a form—it’s your first pitch to the admissions team. In this student-led video, Yashovardhan, a senior at Masters’ Union, shares the ultimate blueprint to crafting a stellar application—from structuring essays to avoiding common pitfalls.

01.

Complete the
Application

01.

Complete the
Application

Requirements

Fill out your details

You will need:

  • Your latest resume - Applicants are allowed to choose their own format (needs to be clear and concise). Feel free to use our sample resume format.

  • Your qualification documents - Upload all documents related to your work experience, academics and extra-curriculars to support your application.

Upload your CAT/GMAT/GMAT Focus score

You may submit your CAT/GMAT/GMAT Focus scores in your application.

02.

MU-BAAT
(Masters’ Union Business Aptitude & Admissions Test)

02.

MU-BAAT (Masters’ Union Business Aptitude & Admissions Test)

Requirements

About the Online Scholarship Test

Applicants who do not have a valid CAT or GMAT score (or if you feel your score does not reflect your true potential) are required to take the MU-BAAT. If you have submitted a CAT or GMAT score and are happy with it, then you are not required to take the MU-BAAT.

The MU-BAAT is designed to evaluate:

  • Your communication and business aptitude

  • Clarity of thought and idea structuring

  • Creative and analytical thinking

You record your responses online and do not need any prior preparation. Think of the questions as a conversation with the Masters’ Union team that helps us understand you beyond your résumé.

Duration: 45-60 minutes

Format: 9 questions divided into 5 sections. The test starts with a simple, fun question and gradually moves to questions focused on your story, motivation, creativity, and analytical thinking. You will receive short breaks between sections.

Test Window:

After you submit the application and pay the application fee, our admissions team will evaluate your profile and invite you to take the MU-BAAT. You will receive a link from us typically 1-2 weeks after you submit your application. You will then have 14 days to take the test.

Sample Questions:

  • If you could shadow any business leader for one day, who would it be and what would you aim to learn?

  • A D2C skincare brand increased their ad spend, but average order value fell 20%, while new users grew. What do you think happened?

03.

Schedule In-Person
Marketing Simulation & Interview

03.

Schedule In-Person Marketing Simulation & Interview

Requirements

About In-Person Business Simulation and Interview

Following a comprehensive evaluation, shortlisted applicants will receive an invitation to proceed to the interview stage.

  • If selected, applicants will be informed of the interview date, venue, and time 3–5 days prior to the interview.

  • The interviews will be conducted by distinguished faculty members and seasoned industry leaders.

04.

Admission
Decision

04.

Admission Decision

Requirements

Status of Evaluation

Based on the final decision of the admissions committee, applicants will be slotted into the following categories-

  • Applicants who have been offered admission to Masters’ Union will receive their decision along with the financial structure for the upcoming programme.

  • For applicants who are waitlisted, the admissions committee will reconvene and promote candidates from the waitlisted to the accepted pool, on a regular basis. Please note that we do not maintain a ranked waitlist. Your admission is dependent on the spots that might open up later.

  • If you’ve received a rejection email, you cannot apply in any subsequent round in the same academic year. You are welcome to re-apply to the programme in the next academic year.

Fee Structure

Rounds partially-Refundable
Admission Fee

Tuition Fee

GST will not be applicable on tuition and admission fees, as we are registered with the National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC).

Investing in Your Future

100%

Maximum merit-based programme fee received by a student.

90+

Scholarships available for the postgraduate batch.

100%

Loan coverage for the programme fee.

  • Manoj Kohli Merit-based Scholarship
  • Rajni Thethi Scholarship for Women
  • Chairman’s Diversity Scholarship
  • MU Scholarship for Technology Leaders
  • P. Dwarakanath India-bound Scholarship
  • Pankaj Bansal Scholarship for Young Leaders
  • Founders’ Bursary for Entrepreneurs
  • Siblings Scholarship
  • Bharat Innovators Scholarship

    • Manoj Kohli Merit-based Scholarship

    The Manoj Kohli Merit-based Scholarship extends financial support to candidates with exceptional academic records and professional accomplishments. These scholarships are offered as partial waivers of the tuition fee, as decided by the admissions committee.

    Rajni Thethi Scholarship for Women

    The Rajni Thethi Scholarship for Women extends support to deserving female candidates with impressive academic and/or professional track record, along with the drive and potential to emerge as an industry leader.

    Chairman’s Diversity Scholarship

    The Chairman’s Diversity Scholarship aims at the inclusion of exceptional candidates from marginalised and underserved communities from the Indian subcontinent, such as the Northeast, Ladakh, Bhutan, and the tribal belts of the Indian hinterland.

    MU Scholarship for Technology Leaders

    The MU Scholarship for Technology Leaders is for candidates who have demonstrated technology-oriented leadership either as students or in their jobs, through innovations, patents or research, and are looking to further their career prospects in the technology domain.

    P. Dwarakanath India-bound Scholarship

    The P. Dwarakanath India-bound Scholarship is directed at Indian-origin candidates (i.e. PIO card-holders) who wish to engage with the Indian corporate sector, as well as Indian professionals currently based abroad, who wish to return to India to pursue world-class education and catapult their careers to newer heights.

    Pankaj Bansal Scholarship for Young Leaders

    The Pankaj Bansal Scholarship for Young Leaders aims to identify young professionals who have displayed exceptional drive and leadership potential through their professional projects, community service and active participation in student-led committees, clubs, and other initiatives while in college.

    Founder’s Bursary for Entrepreneurs

    The Founders’ Bursary for Entrepreneurs funds candidates who have had entrepreneurial experience, with varying levels of success, and wish to gain more skills and academic perspectives to carry on with their line of work.

    Siblings Scholarship

    The Siblings Scholarship offers financial support to candidates who have siblings in the current or previous cohorts of Masters’ Union. This scholarship recognises the continued trust placed in us by families and celebrates their shared journey within the Masters’ Union community. Awards are offered as partial waivers of the tuition fee, as decided by the admissions committee.

    Bharat Innovators Scholarship

    The Bharat Innovators Scholarship is aimed at young Indian entrepreneurs from non-metropolitan towns and villages who have launched a startup or built a minimum viable product (MVP). This scholarship supports grassroots innovators looking to scale their ventures while gaining access to world-class education, mentorship, and a thriving startup ecosystem.

    Manoj Kohli Merit-Based Scholarship

    The Manoj Kohli Merit-based Scholarship extends financial support to candidates with exceptional academic records and professional accomplishments. These scholarships are offered as partial waivers of the tuition fee, as decided by the admissions committee.

    Rajni Thethi Scholarship for Women

    The Rajni Thethi Scholarship for Women extends support to deserving female candidates with impressive academic and/or professional track record, along with the drive and potential to emerge as an industry leader.

    Chairman’s Diversity Scholarship

    The Chairman’s Diversity Scholarship aims at the inclusion of exceptional candidates from marginalised and underserved communities from the Indian subcontinent, such as the Northeast, Ladakh, Bhutan, and the tribal belts of the Indian hinterland.

    MU Scholarship For Technology Leaders

    The MU Scholarship for Technology Leaders is for candidates who have demonstrated technology-oriented leadership either as students or in their jobs, through innovations, patents or research, and are looking to further their career prospects in the technology domain.

    P. Dwarakanath India-bound Scholarship

    The P. Dwarakanath India-bound Scholarship is directed at Indian-origin candidates (i.e. PIO card-holders) who wish to engage with the Indian corporate sector, as well as Indian professionals currently based abroad, who wish to return to India in pursuit of a world-class education, that may help catapult their careers to newer heights.

    Pankaj Bansal Scholarship For Young Leaders

    The Pankaj Bansal Scholarship for Young Leaders aims to identify young professionals who have displayed exceptional drive and leadership potential through their professional projects, community service and active participation in student-led committees, clubs, and other initiatives while in college.

    Founders’ Bursary For Entrepreneurs

    The Founders’ Bursary for Entrepreneurs funds candidates who have had entrepreneurial experience, with varying levels of success, and wish to gain more skills and academic perspectives to carry on with their line of work.

    Siblings Scholarship

    The Siblings Scholarship offers financial support to candidates who have siblings in the current or previous cohorts of Masters’ Union. This scholarship recognises the continued trust placed in us by families and celebrates their shared journey within the Masters’ Union community. Awards are offered as partial waivers of the tuition fee, as decided by the admissions committee.

    Bharat Innovators Scholarship

    The Bharat Innovators Scholarship is aimed at young Indian entrepreneurs from non-metropolitan towns and villages who have launched a startup or built a minimum viable product (MVP). This scholarship supports grassroots innovators looking to scale their ventures while gaining access to world-class education, mentorship, and a thriving startup ecosystem.

    Job Prospects

    • People Operations & Culture Roles

      Opportunities in People Ops, culture building, and HR analytics across startups
      and tech firms.

    • Enterprise HR Functions

      Roles in employer branding, L&D, talent strategy, and organisational development
      in large companies.

    • Leadership & Strategic HR

      Leadership programmes and CHRO office roles across top organisations.

    Consulting Careers

    • HR Consulting

      Roles in HR consulting within Big 4 and specialised boutique firms.

    • HR Tech & Transformation

      Projects focused on HR tech systems, digital transformation, and workforce redesign.

    • Organisational Design Exposure

      Experience in digital HR solutions, structure redesign, and change management.

    Other Benefits

    • Dedicated Career Pathways

       A focused track for HR and organisational strategy careers.

    • Mentorship & Industry Access

      Guidance from CHROs, consultants, and founders across leading organisations.

    • Skill Building

      Develop competencies in people analytics, change management, and strategic leadership.

    industry with Mr. Naveen Munjal, MD of Hero Electric.

    Discover the impact of Gen-AI on content creation & how you
    can leverage AI to transform your career with Varun Mayya.

    Karan Johar, Filmmaker & Entrepreneur

    Learn how India’s top filmmaker built Dharma navigating budgets, box office risks, the business behind blockbusters.

    Kunal Bahl, Former CEO, Snapdeal

    Survive early-stage chaos, time your launch right & build a ₹100 crore business without chasing funding.

    Why HR & OS, Why Now?

    The world of work is transforming faster than ever. With AI, hybrid teams, and global
    mobility reshaping organisations, HR has become a core driver of business strategy.
    India produces ~5 lakh MBAs, yet under 5% specialise in HR, despite growing
    demand for leaders skilled in analytics, culture, and people strategy. Globally, the
    CHRO is now the #2 most in-demand C-suite role after CTO, reflecting HR’s rising
    importance.

    The Masters’ Union PGP in Human Resources & Organisation Strategy (HR&OS)
    develops future-ready HR leaders trained in business fundamentals, people
    analytics, and real-world organisational design to shape the future of work.

    Explore Programme

    Frequently Asked
    Questions

    • Programme Overview
    • Eligibility and Criteria
    • Learning and Outcomes
    • Admission Policies

    What is a PG Programme in Human Resources & Organisation Strategy (HR&OS)?

    A 16-month full-time postgraduate programme blending Masters’ Union’s business foundation with hands-on specialisations in human capital, organisational design, leadership, and people analytics.

    What will I learn in the Programme?

    Learn to build people strategies, design organisations, and lead talent in a fast-changing business environment. Learn directly from top faculty, CHROs, CXOs, and founders through live case studies, mentorship, and industry-led workshops. Our “learn by doing” curriculum shapes future HR leaders at the intersection of people, strategy, culture, and analytics.

    What is the program Structure?

    The curriculum spans 8 terms across 16 months

    • Terms 1–2 building core business foundations

    • Terms 3–5 mastering human resources & organisation strategy

    • Term 6-8: Capstone and consulting projects with real organisations

    What will be the batch size of the class?

    We will limit the batch size to a maximum of 60 students to ensure personalised learning, close mentorship from masters, and a highly effective teacher–student ratio.

    What is the eligibility criteria?

    The PGP in Human Resources & Organisation Strategy is open to candidates who have completed their undergraduate degree or will graduate by April 2026 onwards. Ideal applicants include fresh graduates, early-career professionals (0–5 years), and professionals aspiring to build careers in people, culture, and organisational leadership.

    How do I apply?

    Click Apply Now (tag the application now link) on our website, complete the online application form, and our admissions team will reach out to you at the earliest possible time.

    Are scholarships available for the PGP HR&OS programme?

    Yes, scholarships are available for candidates with strong leadership potential, prior people management exposure, and demonstrated impact in organisational or HR-related initiatives

    How can I avail the scholarship?

    While completing your application, you can express interest in available scholarships. Shortlisted candidates will be asked to submit supporting documents or short essays to confirm their eligibility.

    If an application has been unsuccessful, may they reapply in the next round?

    No, candidates cannot reapply in the next round. They may reapply for the next intake in the following academic year by submitting a new online application. It’s advisable to do so only if there have been significant updates or improvements to their profile since the previous application.

    Will I get to network with Industry leaders?

    Yes, you will learn and engage with top faculties, CHROs, people leaders, founders, and CXOs across diverse industries.

    Can I work on my own startup ideas during the programme?

    Absolutely. The programme fosters entrepreneurial thinking and provides ample opportunities to apply classroom and immersion learnings to your own ventures.

    Are the fees refunded in case applicants decide to drop out of the programme?

    Fees once paid are non-refundable. Masters’ Union reserves the right to modify this refund policy at any time without prior notice.

    Are the fees refunded in case applicants decide to drop out of the programme?

    Fees once paid are non-refundable. Masters’ Union reserves the right to modify this refund policy at any time without prior notice.

    Can applicants defer my admission offer from Masters' Union to the next year?

    At present, we do not offer the option to defer admission. Candidates unable to join the programme this year are encouraged to reapply for the next intake.
